faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth problem in my Great Kills home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small irregular holes in wool clothing, cashmere, or upholstery; silken tubes or cases in closets; adult moths flying near wardrobes; and a musty odor in storage areas. If you notice any of these indicators in your Great Kills home, professional inspection is recommended to assess the extent of the infestation.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth treatment cost in Great Kills?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in Great Kills typically ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your property, and the number of affected areas. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes with no obligation, ensuring transparent pricing for all our Great Kills customers.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Most treatments show immediate results with adult moth elimination, but complete eradication requires addressing eggs and larvae, which can take 2-4 weeks. We typically recommend follow-up treatments at 2-3 week intervals to break the reproductive cycle and ensure all life stages are eliminated from your Great Kills property.

faqs.question: Can I prevent clothes moths from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes! We provide comprehensive prevention guidance including regular vacuuming of closets, proper storage in airtight containers, cedar products for natural deterrence, and periodic inspections. Our Great Kills clients also benefit from seasonal maintenance plans that keep moths from re-establishing in your home.
